title: GST/HST in specific situations - Canada.ca
content:
## GST/HST in specific situations
The following chart provides links to information on how GST/HST applies in specific situations.
Information on what to do when you have to charge the GST/HST and are never paid for it.
Calculating the GST/HST in special cases
Information on how the GST/HST applies to different transactions, including coupons, deposits, gift cards and certificates, trade-ins, tips, volume discounts, returnable containers, and more.
Charities - General information
Information on how the GST/HST applies to charities including registration requirements, exemptions, tax calculations, and rebates.
Charities - How to calculate your net tax
Information on how to calculate your net tax as a charity and how to fill out your GST/HST return.
Information on how the GST/HST applies if you are a builder, land developer, renovator, contractor, or other person involved in the home construction industry.
Conventions: sponsors, organizers, and exhibitors
Information on the GST/HST rebate for sponsors of foreign conventions, organizers of foreign conventions, and non-resident exhibitors.
Customers outside Canada - GST/HST - Import and exports
Information on how the GST/HST applies to imports and exports and the export programs that provide GST/HST relief.
Customers outside your province or territory - GST/HST and place-of-supply rules
Information on how to determine which province a supply is made in, which tax rate applies, how to pay or recover the provincial part of the HST, and more.
Information on the GST/HST and electronic commerce.
Employees and partners - GST/HST rebate
Information on how the GST/HST applies to a member of a partnership and the GST/HST rebate.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
